hard.next_step: POC planning meeting held 3 Jul with Domino's Europe team; next step is Domino's providing indirect spend data for the Spend POC, with data-availability across disparate ERPs still being checked.
hard.ais_why: CPO Jai Rastogi is driving an ANA (autonomous negotiation) plus spend-analysis POC to prove quick value to internal stakeholders, with CLM as Phase 2; buyer-led AI demand with an active champion, formal POC execution still pending.

north_star_verdict.headline: Healthy, buyer-driven POC-stage deal with a strong CPO champion and clear pain, but the 28 Aug close is not credible: the POC has not started, data is still being gathered across disparate ERPs, the CFO is not yet engaged, and commercials and contracting are all still ahead.
north_star_verdict.math: Created 22 Apr 2026, ~75 days in Formal Evaluation. Close date 28 Aug is 53 days out. POC scope defined 10 Jun (indirect spend across seven markets, spend analysis plus ANA) but as of 3 Jul the buyer is still identifying what data is extractable from Navision and other ERPs, so the POC has not begun. A Zycus EU lead (Michael Gengenbach) was brought on 1 to 2 Jul and a platform overview ran 3 Jul, resetting POC prep. Remaining path: data load, POC execution (2 to 3 weeks minimum), internal cross-functional sign-off, CFO Lynn Carruthers approval, commercials, and contracting (MSA/Order Form/SOW). 53 days is insufficient. Deal is very live: buyer emails through 2 Jul, platform session 3 Jul, full demo 3 Jun. Forward slip to Q4 2026 probable.
evidence: Deal is live and buyer-engaged: 3 Jul platform overview / POC-focus session with the Domino's Europe team (Michael Gengenbach, Danny, Maria Oehler), active POC-scope email thread 25 Jun to 2 Jul with Jai, and a full platform demo 3 Jun

POC scope crystallized 10 Jun: cover indirect spend data across seven markets, focus on spend analysis (iAnalyze) plus the autonomous negotiation agent (ANA/Anna), prove AI-led savings identification before expanding to other modules and CLM

Champion confirmed and active: Jai Rastogi (Group CPO) is driving the eval, set the demo agenda, structured the POC, delegated data-gathering to AU and EU owners, and is personally close to Carl Kimball; gave Zycus 'a front run before we do anything formal' (29 May)

Strong quantified motivation (Jai, 3 Jun): share price fell from $161 to $16.68 through hyperinflation with no menu-board increases; the turnaround must be funded by procurement savings, and 'the fuel to get fit for growth comes from this table'
Economic Buyer mapped but not engaged: Lynn Carruthers (CFO) is the Executive Sponsor contact role and has appeared on no call; POC ROI is the gate to her budget sign-off

POC execution risk surfaced 3 Jul: 12 markets with 12 different system setups, some with no ERP, Germany on D365 in hypercare; Maria Oehler flagged the data ask as a worry and recommended limiting the first cut to one indirect category
Commercial signal: Jai asked Luke on 10 Jun to prepare the most aggressive commercial pricing possible to help the internal sell, and the amount was raised from $200k to $450k on 6 Jun
Pipeline forecast is indefensible at this stage and timeline; Best Case is the honest category given the active POC and strong champion, but the 28 Aug date will slip to Q4 2026

metrics.narrative: Strong qualitative and macro-quantified pain, with the solution-specific ROI still to be proven on the POC. Jai Rastogi (Group CPO) framed the whole initiative on 3 Jun as funding the company turnaround: share price fell from $161 to $16.68 after hyperinflation with no menu-board increases, and 'the fuel to get fit for growth and generate cash for reinvestment comes from this table' (procurement). Inefficiency was quantified in time and error: mapping technology spend 'has taken almost 8 weeks' with no full answer, and a CEO spend question remains unanswered '9 months later'. Zycus illustrated savings magnitude in the demo (supplier rationalization, TCO analysis, market-benchmarked negotiation), but these are Zycus examples, not yet Domino's validated numbers. Quantifying Domino's real savings on their own 7-market indirect data is precisely the stated POC goal, so the metric is confirmed as a need and the move is to make the POC output the dollar case.

economic_buyer.narrative: The budget owner is identified but not engaged. Lynn Carruthers (CFO) is the named Executive Sponsor contact role and, given Jai's framing that the turnaround must be funded from procurement savings, she is the person who signs off the enterprise spend. She has appeared on no call to date. Jai Rastogi (Group CPO) controls the procurement mandate and is actively driving the eval and pricing conversation (he asked Luke on 10 Jun for aggressive commercial pricing 'to help the internal sell'), so he is the operational buyer, but the POC ROI is the gate to Lynn's formal approval. The move is to convert POC savings proof into a CFO exec value session tied to the cost-out / fit-for-growth narrative before commercials, so budget authority is secured rather than assumed.
sources: SF OpportunityContactRole: Lynn Carruthers, CFO, Executive Sponsor

decision_criteria.narrative: The evaluation criteria are explicit and buyer-set through the POC design. Jai scoped two capabilities to prove: (1) spend analysis (iAnalyze/Merlin) that ingests and normalizes messy, unstructured indirect spend from disparate ERPs and surfaces savings opportunities, and (2) the autonomous negotiation agent (Anna/ANA) for tactical, low-value indirect spend. On the 3 Jul session the buyer probed the real criteria: whether the agent can handle bilateral (single-supplier) as well as competitive negotiations (Michael Gengenbach), whether it can integrate beyond spend data to SharePoint/contracts/email (Michael), non-English vendor languages for European suppliers (Maria Oehler), and whether raw multi-ERP data can be normalized without heavy manual cleansing (Danny). Ease of use via the Microsoft Teams front end was positioned as a criterion. Criteria are confirmed; the open question the POC must answer is data feasibility on their own messy extracts.
sources: Avoma platform overview 3 Jul (Michael Gengenbach, Maria Oehler, Danny)

decision_process.narrative: Jai is running a deliberate prove-then-expand process, readable directly from the call sequence. He took a broad 2-hour platform demo (3 Jun, Brisbane HQ, with Annie Doyle, Fran and team), then said 'let's take a step back and do a proof of concept on a very specific problem' (indirect tail spend) to 'give an internal business justification to move ahead' across all markets and modules. He then delegated data-gathering to named AU and EU owners (25 Jun to 3 Jul), pulling in his Europe team (Michael Gengenbach leading, Danny in finance, Maria Oehler on global projects) for the 3 Jul overview. The approval chain after a successful POC runs: procurement internal sign-off (Jai) → CFO Lynn Carruthers budget approval → commercials → contracting; CIO Wayne McMahon is likely required for Dynamics/ERP integration. No formal RFP or committee vote is in play.
sources: Tasks 25 Jun to 2 Jul POC thread

paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics are early but progressing cleanly. A mutual NDA was signed and countersigned 26 to 27 May (Kadie Skehan, DPE procurement) specifically to allow internal data sharing for the POC, which unblocked the platform sessions. As a full-suite new-business deal (S2C plus ANA), the paper ahead is a Master Service Agreement plus Order Form plus a separately signed SOW, with CLM as a Phase 2 legal workstream. There is no incumbent-contract expiration acting as a forcing function (this is a greenfield, low-technology-enablement environment), so the timeline is driven by POC outcome and internal budget timing rather than a renewal clock. Visibility would improve by mapping the procurement/legal signatory path and sequencing commercials against a defined POC end date.
sources: Task 26 to 27 May NDA thread (Kadie Skehan)
Avoma platform overview 3 Jul (Michael Gengenbach confirmed full NDA in place)

identify_pain.narrative: The operational pain is specific, confirmed and owned by the buyer. Jai Rastogi described an enterprise procurement function that is 'very immature' with 'no enablement at all', just launching Dynamics as its ERP and with 'no other source-to-contract capabilities here at all' (3 Jun). Indirect spend across 12 markets is essentially unmanaged, with no common taxonomy and inconsistent supplier naming across disparate or absent ERPs, which his own team confirmed causes constant misclassification (3 Jul, Danny and Maria on duplicate vendor entities across Germany/Benelux/France). Contracts sit outside any workflow, compliance is low, and leakage is significant. The pain owner is the group procurement function under Jai, with the indirect / IT-procurement leads (Fran, Sarah, Michael Gengenbach) feeling it operationally. The pain is directly tied to the company turnaround, giving it urgency.
sources: Avoma demo 3 Jun, Jai Rastogi and Fran
Avoma platform overview 3 Jul (Danny, Maria Oehler)

champion.narrative: Jai Rastogi (Group Chief Procurement and Supply Chain Officer) is a strong, active champion. He drove the demo request (5 May), set the agenda, led buyer participation on the 3 Jun demo, structured the POC, sent clarification questions on POC metrics (6 Jun), asked Luke to prepare aggressive commercial pricing to help the internal sell (10 Jun), delegated data-gathering to his AU and EU teams, and is personally close to Carl Kimball (ex Thai Union). His advocacy is explicit: 'we're going to do a POC with Zycus' and 'you've got a front run before we do anything formal' (29 May). What is still developing is the coalition above and beside him: the CFO (Lynn Carruthers) and CIO (Wayne McMahon) have not joined a call, and the newly engaged EU lead Michael Gengenbach is curious but not yet an advocate (skeptical whether agentic negotiation adoption 'is really there' on 3 Jul). The deal is single-threaded at the power level despite broad procurement engagement. Current engagement is active as of 3 Jul.

competition.narrative: This is a sole-sourced deal whose real opponent is Do Nothing / the status quo of spreadsheets, Jira and manual processes. Jai stated 'I haven't gone out to market to anyone' and gave Zycus 'a front run before we do anything formal' (29 May); no RFP is running. Jai carries prior deployment experience with Coupa and SAP Ariba (at Thai Union and other organizations) and has evaluated Ivalua, so he benchmarks Zycus against best-of-breed, but none of those vendors is engaged on this opportunity, so they are dormant reference points, not active threats. The wedge is Zycus AI (Merlin plus the Anna negotiation agent) for agentic opportunity discovery and normalization of disparate-ERP data with no common taxonomy, delivered as a fast, modular POC that funds the wider transformation. The genuine risk is not a rival vendor but a weak or slow POC that fails to quantify savings and lets internal momentum revert to status quo, or eventually triggers a formal RFP to the vendors Jai already knows.

deal_movement.summary: Momentum is buyer-led and holding: the eval moved from a broad 3 Jun demo into a scoped POC, and on 1 to 3 Jul Jai pulled in his Europe team and Zycus ran a platform overview to start POC data prep. The deal is still pre-POC-execution, with data availability the live gate.

items.change: 3 Jul platform overview / POC-focus session held with Domino's Europe team (Michael Gengenbach, Danny, Maria Oehler); Zycus walked spend analysis and the Anna negotiation agent and set the data-gathering next step
Michael Gengenbach (EU indirect-spend lead) brought in to lead the POC from Europe; demo re-run for him after he missed the 3 Jun session
Jai delegated POC data-gathering to an EU team member plus his AU team while travelling
Amount raised from $200k to $450k (by Carl Kimball)
POC scope confirmed: indirect spend across seven markets, spend analysis first then ANA (Jai/Luke)
Close date pulled in from 30 Oct to 28 Aug (by Luke Dougherty)
Mutual NDA signed and countersigned to enable internal data sharing (Kadie Skehan, DPE)

competitive_position.summary: No active vendor competition. The primary competitor is Do Nothing (status quo: spreadsheets, Jira, manual processes across 12 markets). Jai Rastogi (CPO) has prior experience deploying Coupa and SAP Ariba at Thai Union and evaluated Ivalua, but stated 'I haven't gone out to market to anyone' and 'you've got a front run' before any formal RFP (29 May). Zycus is sole-sourced and the deal is ours to lose. The strongest current threat is reversion to Do Nothing if POC results are weak or data-quality issues across disparate ERPs prevent clear savings quantification, which would stall internal momentum and could later trigger a formal RFP to Coupa or Ariba.

competitors.name: Do Nothing (status quo)
competitors.quote: Procurement maturity is exceptionally low. Bodies in spreadsheets. No source-to-contract technology. No CLM system, poor contract compliance, significant leakage. Indirect spend completely unmanaged.
competitors.how_we_win: Deliver strong POC ROI on their own disparate-ERP indirect spend using Merlin to normalize data and surface savings, show quick wins, secure CFO sponsorship, and move before momentum reverts to status quo.

competitors.name: Coupa
competitors.quote: I've worked with everyone from Ivalua to Coupa to Ariba. I've deployed Coupa and Ariba at Thai Union and other organizations.
competitors.how_we_win: Jai knows Coupa from prior deployments but has not engaged them for this eval; keep the front run by proving a faster AI POC-to-value cycle before any formal RFP opens.

competitors.name: SAP Ariba
competitors.quote: I've worked with everyone from Ivalua to Coupa to Ariba. I've deployed Coupa and Ariba at Thai Union and other organizations.
competitors.how_we_win: Jai knows Ariba from prior deployments but has not engaged them here; win by demonstrating superior AI and faster POC-to-value before a formal RFP.

competitors.name: Ivalua
competitors.quote: I've worked with everyone from Ivalua to Coupa to Ariba.
competitors.how_we_win: Jai evaluated Ivalua in prior roles but has not engaged them here; keep the front run by proving quick, AI-led savings on their own data.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Strong strategic fit on AI-led spend visibility and tactical negotiation; the exposure is on data feasibility across disparate ERPs and European-specific requirements the POC still has to satisfy.

best_practice_check.summary: Sole-sourced, buyer-led POC deal that is ours to lose; the win levers are securing the CFO before results land, pre-defining the savings bar with Jai, and containing POC scope so data quality does not stall momentum.
flags: Multi-thread to CFO Lynn Carruthers now, via Jai, tied to the cost-out / fit-for-growth turnaround story: power access is single-threaded on Jai and she gates the budget.
Pre-agree the POC success metric and savings target with Jai so CFO sign-off criteria are defined before results, de-risking the ROI gate.
Contain the first POC cut to one indirect category (packaging, as Maria Oehler recommended) so data quality across 12 disparate ERPs does not stall momentum.

Genuine AI appetite, not over-rated.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: Sole-sourced, CPO-driven POC deal: Jai Rastogi gave Zycus 'a front run' before any RFP and is funding a turnaround from procurement savings; the only things between us and the win are proving savings on messy 7-market indirect data and engaging CFO Lynn Carruthers before the 28 Aug date.

win_position.text: Zycus is the only vendor Jai Rastogi (Group CPO) has engaged: he skipped going to market, gave us 'a front run before we do anything formal' (29 May), and personally structured the POC and pricing ask, so the deal is ours to lose.
Power access is single-threaded on Jai: CFO Lynn Carruthers, who owns the turnaround budget, has appeared on no call and the POC ROI is the gate to her sign-off (SF Executive Sponsor, no activity).
The win hinges on proving savings on genuinely messy data across 12 markets with disparate or missing ERPs (Germany on D365 in hypercare); Maria Oehler flagged the data ask as a worry on 3 Jul.
AI appetite and pain are strong: AIS 8 (Strong); Jai tied the initiative to a share-price collapse from $161 to $16.68 and wants Merlin and Anna to accelerate savings (3 Jun).

deal_momentum.text: Buyer-led cadence held through 3 Jul: Jai pulled in his EU team (Michael Gengenbach, Danny, Maria Oehler) and Zycus ran a platform overview / POC-focus session that day.
Amount grew from $200k to $450k on 6 Jun and commercials entered the room when Jai asked Luke for aggressive pricing on 10 Jun.
The POC still has not started: as of 3 Jul the buyer is checking what indirect spend data is extractable from Navision and other ERPs.

customer_commitment.text: NDA countersigned 27 May (Kadie Skehan) to allow internal data sharing, and Jai delegated data-gathering to named AU and EU owners (25 Jun to 3 Jul).
Jai committed the POC as the internal business justification to expand to all modules and CLM Phase 2 (3 Jun).
Commitment is concentrated in procurement: no finance or IT executive has committed time and the required data has not yet been produced.

deal_risk.text: The 28 Aug close is not credible: data load, 2-3 week POC execution, internal sign-off, CFO approval and contracting cannot complete in 53 days, so a slip to Q4 2026 is likely.
Data quality across 12 disparate or absent ERPs could stall the POC; containing scope to one indirect category (packaging) is the mitigation Maria raised on 3 Jul.
Do-nothing inertia is the real opponent: a weak POC lets momentum revert to spreadsheets and Jira or triggers a formal RFP to Coupa or Ariba that Jai knows from prior roles.

timeline.evidence: 28 Aug close not credible; POC not started as of 3 Jul with data still being scoped

executive_alignment.evidence: CFO Lynn Carruthers (budget owner) has joined no call; single-threaded on Jai

critical_signals.text: Sole-source: Jai skipped an RFP and gave Zycus a front run, so the real opponent is status-quo inertia while the turnaround needs procurement savings to fund it.

The 3 Jul platform overview restarted POC prep with the Europe team, but Maria Oehler flagged the data ask as a worry and Michael Gengenbach doubted agentic negotiation adoption is 'really there' yet.
Michael Gengenbach, the EU indirect-spend lead, now owns the POC data from Europe and is freshly engaged but curious rather than convinced, so he is not yet an advocate.
Jai asked for aggressive pricing on 10 Jun to build the internal case and the amount rose to $450k on 6 Jun, but ROI must be proven on the POC before CFO Lynn Carruthers signs.
European data hosting for a group agreement, raised by Fran on 3 Jun, is still an open item Zycus committed to answer.

expansion_context.note: New-business greenfield: DPE has 'no enablement at all', just launching Dynamics, NDA freshly signed; no prior Zycus Closed-Won on this account, so executive access must be earned, not inherited.

customer_preference.evidence: Jai gave Zycus 'a front run', is driving the POC and pricing himself, and has not gone to any other vendor (29 May to 3 Jul)

business_case.evidence: Strong macro and qualitative pain quantified (share price $161 to $16.68, 8-week spend-mapping, 9-month unanswered CEO question), but Domino's own solution ROI is not yet validated - that is the stated POC goal
